title: WellNet
description: Client-side stability helper for heavy modpacks: monitors connection/session health, smooths world joins, and adapts safe client settings for steadier Forge/Fabric gameplay.
body:
# WellNet

WellNet is a lightweight client-side helper for players who want smoother Minecraft sessions in heavy modpacks. It watches connection and session health in the background, then uses safe client-side adjustments to keep the game responsive during world joins, chunk-heavy moments, and local hosting sessions.

## What WellNet does

- Monitors session stability while you play.
- Watches for lag spikes, sudden load surges, and rough join moments.
- Smooths the first seconds after entering a world, when modpacks often stutter the most.
- Helps local hosts keep the client calmer while friends are connected.
- Uses separate Forge and Fabric files, so you can pick the exact jar for your Minecraft version.
- Gives a clearer `/wellnet status` output so you can quickly understand what the mod is doing.

## Current downloads

Use the file that matches both your Minecraft version and your mod loader.

| WellNet | Minecraft | Forge | Fabric |
|---|---:|:---:|:---:|
| 1.2.0 | 1.20.4 | yes | yes |
| 1.2.0 | 1.20.1 | yes | yes |
| 1.2.0 | 1.19.2 | yes | yes |
| 1.1.0 | 1.20.1 | yes | yes |
| 1.0.0 | 1.20.1 | yes | no |

## Good fit for

- Large modpacks with random short freezes.
- Players who want a plug-and-play stability helper without tuning menus.
- Local worlds hosted for friends.
- Forge or Fabric setups where you want a small background mod focused on smoother sessions.

## Command

- `/wellnet status` shows the current WellNet state in plain language.

- [Source code](https://github.com/wellqx/WellNet)
